prompt: {{qtext[qi]}} Tap the stage on the curve.

derived.qtext: ['Two senior members just left and were replaced by contractors; old conflicts have resurfaced and velocity has dropped. Which stage has the team most likely regressed to?', 'The team has just been assembled — members are polite and eager but unclear on goals, roles and how decisions get made. Which stage are they in?', 'Friction has largely settled: the team now has stable norms, trust and shared working agreements. Which stage is this?', 'The team is self-organising, resolving issues without the PM, and delivering at its highest output. Which stage is this?', 'The work is finished; the team is capturing lessons learned and being released to other projects. Which stage is this?']
derived.whys: ['Renewed conflict and role re-negotiation after a membership change is a regression to Storming.', 'Forming: polite and eager but unsure of goals, roles and decisions, and dependent on the leader.', 'Norming: the team agrees how it will work — trust and shared agreements settle in.', 'Performing: the team works effectively with little friction at peak output.', 'Adjourning (mourning): closure, lessons learned, and release of the team.']

items.label: st[0]
items.why: i == ans ? whys[qi] : (st[0] + ' is a different stage.')

explanation: Tuckman's stages run Forming → Storming → Norming → Performing → Adjourning. {{whys[qi]}}

prompt: On this conflict-handling grid (assertiveness vs cooperativeness), which mode sits at the marked point?
caption: Axes only — the modes are not labelled.

derived.descs: ['high assertiveness, low cooperation','high assertiveness and high cooperation — win/win','low assertiveness and low cooperation','low assertiveness, high cooperation','moderate on both axes']

explanation: Thomas-Kilmann maps five modes by assertiveness and cooperativeness. The marked point is {{descs[mi]}} → {{names[mi]}}.

prompt: On this salience model, what type of stakeholder is at the marked spot?
caption: Power · Legitimacy · Urgency (overlaps unlabelled).

derived.descs: ['power only','legitimacy only','urgency only','power + legitimacy','power + urgency','legitimacy + urgency','all three (highest priority)']

items.why: s == corr ? ('The marked spot sits in: ' + descs[s] + '.') : ''

explanation: Salience combines power, legitimacy and urgency. The marked region is {{descs[corr]}} → a {{names[corr]}} stakeholder.

prompt: {{causes[ci]}} On this fishbone, tap the cause-category branch it belongs to.

derived.causes: ['Investigation shows an operator skipped a required training step.', 'The approval workflow has no quality checkpoint, so errors pass straight through.', 'A machine was found running well out of calibration.', 'Defects appear only on units built from one supplier’s batch of raw parts.', 'Two gauges on the line give inconsistent readings for the same part.', 'High humidity on the floor warped the parts before assembly.']

items.label: g[0]
items.why: g[0] == causeCats[ci] ? (g[0] + ' — that is where this cause belongs.') : (g[0] + ' is a different category.')

explanation: A fishbone groups causes by category (the 6 M's). This cause is a {{causeCats[ci]}} one.
